content.full_text:
2-78-02-
026

February 3, 1978


Ms. Mabel Barquist
San Jose Republican Women, Federated
355 Patton Avenue
San Jose, California 95128

Dear Ms. Barquist:

Marge Nunes of the Secretary of State's Office has referred your
letter of January 9, 1978, to the Fair Political Practices Commission
for response. The Political Reform Act requires that organizations
such as the Republican Women, Federated need only register as a committee if they collect $500 in any calendar year in earmarked funds,
that is funds that are designated for use in making contributions to
political candidates or committees or if they spend $500 in any calendar year in the making of political contributions. It appears
from your letter that since the group's income is raised solely from
dues and an occasional fund raising event, the proceeds of which are
used to pay operating costs and contribute to the county central
committee, they would have no reporting obligation under the Political
Reform Act. Therefore a Statement of Organization (Form 410) need
not be filed with the Secretary of State's Office. The only question
that I might ask is, are the contributions they make to the county
central committee earmarked by them for their use in making contributions or do they have reason to know that a portion will be used for
the purpose of making contributions. If either of the above occur,
then the Republican Women, Federated is a committee when they give
$500 to the central committee or when the central committee contributes
$500 of the funds they receive from the Republican Women, Federated.
If, however, the money contributed to the county central committee is
used for overhead expenses and their ongoing operation, those funds
would not be reportable and would not go towards qualifying the
Republican Women, Federated as a committee.

I trust the above answers your question. Please do not hesitate to
contact us if you require additional assistance.

Very truly yours,


Regina Siciliano-Kutchins
Technical Assistance Program
Coordinator

RSK:bw

San Jose Republican Women, Federated                           355 Patton Ave.
                                                                        San Jose, California
                                                                        95128

                                                                        January 9, 1978


Marge Nunes,
Political Reform Division
Sacramento, Calif.

Dear Ms. Nunes,

After perusal of Form 410, I wonder if our organization
falls within the definition of a committee required to
register as a political committee. Let me outline our
situation:

1. Our income is derived solely from dues and a single
fund-raising event. (Fashion show) We receive no contributions
and solicit no donations, pledges, or other forms of con-
tribution.

2. No funds are earmarked for any candidate, proposition,
or political committee.

3. We do expect to donate in excess of $500 in 1978 to
the county Central Committee and may make other small donations,
all monies coming from the unexpended income derived from
sources listed in item 1 above.

4. We have not in the past and do not intend to support any
candidate directly or indirectly.

Please advise whether in your opinion we should consider our-
selves a political committee, and so register. If so, I
will promptly return Form 410 which you have already sent me.

Thanking you for your assistance, I remain

                                                    Yours truly

                                                    San Jose Republican Women

                                                    [illegible]
                                                    Treasurer

January 19, 1978


Mrs. Mabel Barquist
355 Patton Ave.
San Jose, CA 95128

Dear Mrs. Barquist:

I have forwarded your letter dated January 9, 1978, to the Fair
Political Practice Commission Technical Anaylsis Division. The
letter you receive from the Fair Political Practice Commission
will serve as record for your files if you are not required to
file.

Sincerely,

WILLIAM N. DURLEY
Assistant to the Secretary of State
Elections and Political Reform

[signature]
MARGE NUNEZ
Political Reform

WND:MN:cm
cc: Fair Political Practice Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, CA 95814
Attn: Gina Kutchins

sections.conclusion: The Political Reform Act requires that organizations such as the Republican Women, Federated need only register as a committee if they collect $500 in any calendar year in earmarked funds, that is funds that are designated for use in making contributions to political candidates or committees or if they spend $500 in any calendar year in the making of political contributions. It appears from your letter that since the group's income is raised solely from dues and an occasional fund raising event, the proceeds of which are used to pay operating costs and contribute to the county central committee, they would have no reporting obligation under the Political Reform Act. Therefore, a Statement of Organization (Form 410) need not be filed with the Secretary of State's Office. The only question that I might ask is, are the contributions they make to the county central committee earmarked by them for their use in making contributions or do they have reason to know that a portion will be used for the purpose of making contributions. If either of the above occur, then the Republican Women, Federated is a committee when they give $500 to the central committee or when the central committee contributes $500 of the funds they receive from the Republican Women, Federated. If, however, the money contributed to the county central committee is used for overhead expenses and their ongoing operation, those funds would not be reportable and would not go towards qualifying the Republican Women, Federated as a committee.
sections.question_synthetic: Must the San Jose Republican Women, Federated register as a political committee under the Political Reform Act given that they derive income from dues and fundraising events and plan to donate over $500 to the county central committee?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The organization does not need to register as a committee because its income comes from dues and fundraising for operating costs, not earmarked funds. However, if contributions to the county central committee are earmarked for use in making political contributions, the organization would become a committee upon giving or contributing $500.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains incoming request (January 9, 1978) followed by routing letter (January 19, 1978) and FPPC response (February 3, 1978). All three sections included in analysis. No formal 'QUESTION' or 'CONCLUSION' headers, but content clearly demarcates these sections.

QUESTION: After perusal of Form 410, I wonder if our organization falls within the definition of a committee required to register as a political committee. Let me outline our situation: 1. Our income is derived solely from dues and a single fund-raising event. (Fashion show) We receive no contributions and solicit no donations, pledges, or other forms of contribution. 2. No funds are earmarked for any candidate, proposition, or political committee. 3. We do expect to donate in excess of $500 in 1978 to the county Central Committee and may make other small donations, all monies coming from the unexpended income derived from sources listed in Item 1 above. 4. We have not in the past and do not now intend to support any candidate directly or indirectly. Please advise whether in your opinion we should consider ourselves a political committee, and so register.

CONCLUSION: The Political Reform Act requires that organizations such as the Republican Women, Federated need only register as a committee if they collect $500 in any calendar year in earmarked funds, that is funds that are designated for use in making contributions to political candidates or committees or if they spend $500 in any calendar year in the making of political contributions. It appears from your letter that since the group's income is raised solely from dues and an occasional fund raising event, the proceeds of which are used to pay operating costs and contribute to the county central committee, they would have no reporting obligation under the Political Reform Act. Therefore, a Statement of Organization (Form 410) need not be filed with the Secretary of State's Office. The only question that I might ask is, are the contributions they make to the county central committee earmarked by them for their use in making contributions or do they have reason to know that a portion will be used for the purpose of making contributions. If either of the above occur, then the Republican Women, Federated is a committee when they give $500 to the central committee or when the central committee contributes $500 of the funds they receive from the Republican Women, Federated. If, however, the money contributed to the county central committee is used for overhead expenses and their ongoing operation, those funds would not be reportable and would not go towards qualifying the Republican Women, Federated as a committee.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter responding to San Jose Republican Women, Federated's inquiry about whether they must register as a political committee. The FPPC concludes they need not register as long as their contributions to the county central committee are not earmarked for political contributions.
